item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Necropost.

I second this motion but reccomend you rename the article "Good German Boots" just because it would tickle me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this with its updates. The interview added so much depth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the final note would have hit a lot harder if we had something substantial regarding the actions taken during D- 0127's time in control.

The test logs play a dual role of elaborating on its effect and showcasing progression towards that endpoint, which is nice, but I wouldn't mind that being pared down in favor of a more developed ending. The final segment loses a lot of punch by being a sweeping overview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made some edits to address those points, thank you for reading it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Novote. This is a well-done concept for something so simple (it's almost Series I vibe, and not in a bad way), but the document itself appears a little messy.

+ show block
– hide block

In reading the tests, I can't help but wonder how the test supervisors fell for this long enough for the situation to worsen to this degree. At first, I thought that maybe the tests were how they discovered the boots' effects, but:

Procedure: Researchers were encouraged to interact with D-0127 in order to prevent the need for additional test subjects, while also documenting their interactions to show SCP-3639's effect in a conversational environment.

If they were already trying to "show SCP-3639's effect," then they were already aware of what the effects are, so why did they not recognize that they were being affected by a recognizable pattern of activity? If someone was able to write this document and describe the effects accurately, I don't see how an entire site could be blind to it happening as if there's a noncorporeal hazard wiping out their ability to reason or recall.

1 month after this state of affairs came to be, off-site communication analysts noticed aberrations in Site-30's reports. SCP-3639-1 had instructed the falsification and misrepresentation of on-site activities, which eventually led to noticeable inconsistency.

…And for a whole month, at that? I could see a few days happening, tops. The notion that one individual, boots or no boots, could falsify that much information without even knowing how to do the jobs of Foundation employees in order to fake it accurately enough to slide unnoticed for a month, is not really something I can believe. Even if they are not directly falsifying documentation, their orders given to personnel would still be noticeably uncharacteristic of normal operations well before a month, I feel.

Edit after edits: Shall read again with a fresh view in the morning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Credit to my girlfriend Caro for helping me write this. Coldpost time y'all.

Image credit: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hungarian_soldiers_in_the_Carpathians.jpg
